mapping.a_element: the rotor-router model, a deterministic analogue of random walk in which a particle follows the rotor/arrow at each site
mapping.b_element: the Eulerian walker: a particle that moves along the directions specified by arrows at each site

mapping.a_element: updating (rotating) the rotor direction each time the particle passes through a site, by a fixed rule
mapping.b_element: changing the direction of the arrows at each site according to fixed rules as the particle moves

mapping.a_element: the abelian sandpile / divisible sandpile structure underlying the rotor-router growth model
mapping.b_element: the Abelian Sandpile Model, whose abelian group of particle-addition operators is the same as that of the Eulerian-walker particle additions

mapping.a_element: particle aggregation on the lattice (sites become occupied as particles settle)
mapping.b_element: particle addition/dropping on the lattice with eventual exit (open graphs) driving the dynamics

brief_justification: Paper A's rotor-router model and Paper B's Eulerian walker are the same deterministic process: a particle routed by arrows at each site that are updated by a fixed rule on each visit, and both are explicitly tied to the Abelian Sandpile Model's abelian group structure. The machinery is identical even though A analyzes the aggregate's asymptotic (spherical) shape while B extracts self-organized-criticality exponents from the same group equivalence.
